free_text: RBS and ERD cross-sections and optical model
parameters for the analysis of lithium, boron and
nickel
Accelerator laboratory, university of
Helsinki
University of Jyvaskyla, department of
physics
The charge states were chosen as +2 at energies
below 13.0 MeV and +3 for higher energies for the
lithium ions. For the boron ions the charge stated were
+3 for energies below 19.1 MeV and +4 for higher
energies.
Three surface barrier detectors were applied
simultaneously at laboratory scattering angles of
89-135 degrees.
The sample for the lithium bombardments was made of
100 nm thick gold leaf on which a 60 nm nickel film was
evaporated.  On this structure a 10 nm gold film was
further evaporated to prevent nickel emission from the
gold leaf.  For the boron measurements the gold leaf
substrate was replaced by silicin waffer.  Thin films
of gold, nickel and gold, with thicknesses of 90, 60
and 10 nm were again evaporated on the substrate.
Optical model parameters for lithium and boron.
By kinematically reversing the reaction the ERD data of
Li(Ni,Li)Ni and B(Ni,B)Ni reactions have been
calculated.
Optical model results
Last checking has been done.
